Scientists and are developing a wind turbine that would be to the ground like a kite, but float thousands of meters in the air to capture jet streams’ energy for electricity. 
 Single wind turbines can be purchased by individuals to generate electricity for their home or business. Progressive Field, home of the Cleveland Guardians baseball team in Cleveland, Ohio, U.S., had an enormous vertical-axis wind turbine. The corkscrew-shaped turbine was expected to generate about 40,000 kilowatt-hours per year, roughly the amount of energy needed to power four homes. However, the turbine was removed in 2013 after being damaged.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2020
[TITLE]: Aerotrope | Vergnet GEV HP 1MW wind turbine
[METADATA/INTRO]: Did you know that some wind turbines can lift and lower their nacelles? Here is the Vergnet GEV HP 1MW, part of Aerotrope's blade design portfolio since 2008, when we delivered the wind turbine blades' CAD surface geometry definition for...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Did you know that some wind turbines can lift and lower their nacelles? Here is the Vergnet GEV HP 1MW, part of Aerotrope's blade design portfolio since 2008, when we delivered the wind turbine blades' CAD surface geometry definition for mould manufacture and root transition aerofoils as part of the fairing process. 
 Earthquake and hurricane-proof, it uses guy wires to fly down the upwind part of the nacelle and blades rapidy and easily. With a hub height of 70 m and a rotor diameter of 62 m, this two-bladed wind turbine is designed specifically for transport to some of the remotest terrain with some of the harshest climates in the world. The wider, thicker rotor blades feature a greater strength versus weight- ratio and high elasticity, which results in an increased lifetime.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2016
[TITLE]: Exelon: Mechanical failure led to turbine collapse
[METADATA/INTRO]: Exelon: Mechanical failure led to turbine collapse A failure in the system that controls the blades on a Huron County wind turbine led to “overspeed conditions” and the ultimate collapse of the 396-foot, 485,000-pound structure....
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: The National Weather Service has said the wind speed Feb. 25 was estimated to be between 40 and 45 mph, with isolated gusts of 50 mph. 
 The 396-foot windmill fell between 5 a.m. and 8 a.m. Feb. 25 in a field in Elkton. The $1.5 million turbine was about eight years old and part of Exelon’s Harvest I wind project. 
 No one was injured in the collapse. Otterness said setbacks between occupied structures and wind turbines vary, but they generally are more than twice the tip height of the turbine. After the collapse, Exelon stopped all turbines in the Harvest I wind project for a short time to perform external visual inspections.
